item.title: Iran war raises demand for US fuel, boosting Gulf Coast refining margins
item.description: U.S. Gulf Coast refiners are reaping the strongest margins in years as disruptions to Middle Eastern oil flows from the Iran war raise demand for U.S. fuel exports, вҖӢanalysts and experts said.

item.title: Berkshire unit must face brokerage commission lawsuit despite $250 million settlement, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge вҖӢruled on Thursday that a Berkshire Hathaway unit must face a вҖҢproposed antitrust class action claiming it conspired to inflate commissions that home sellers pay real estate agents, even though its HomeServices of America brokerage settled the same claims for $250 вҖӢmillion in 2024.

item.title: Paramount seals debt commitments backing Warner Bros deal
item.description: Paramount Skydance has syndicated its bridge loan facility and secured permanent financing from a group of вҖӢbanks for its planned $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros вҖҢDiscovery .

item.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
item.description: Tesla investors said вҖӢthe company's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV, which was reported exclusively by Reuters, might boost вҖҢvolumes and address slowing demand, but at the cost of already strained margins.

item.title: McKinsey to contribute $125 million to Purdue bankruptcy over opioid sales advice
item.description: Consulting firm McKinsey & Co has agreed to contribute $125 million to Purdue Pharma's bankruptcy settlement, resolving potential legal claims over advice it provided to Purdue on вҖӢhow to вҖңturbochargeвҖқ sales of its addictive painkiller OxyContin, according to a court вҖҢfiling submitted late Wednesday.

item.title: American Airlines set to resume US flights to Venezuela as soon as April 30
item.description: American Airlines said вҖӢThursday it aims вҖҢto resume U.S. flights вҖӢto Venezuela вҖӢas soon as April вҒ 30 after вҖӢwinning approval from вҖӢthe Transportation Department last month.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Brown-Forman, WSJ reports
item.description: Alcoholic beverage вҖӢmaker Sazerac is exploring вҖҢa deal with Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman , the вҖӢWall Street Journal вҖӢreported on Thursday, citing people вҒ familiar with the вҖӢmatter.

item.title: Airbus delivers 60 aircrafts in March
item.description: Airbus delivered 60 aircrafts in March, bringing first-quarter deliveries to вҖӢ114 jets, down 16% from вҖҢthe same point last year, the European planemaker said on Thursday.

item.title: VW to stop making its top electric vehicle in the US
item.description: Volkswagen said on Thursday it will end production of вҖӢthe ID.4 electric SUV out of вҖҢits Tennessee plant this month, citing a challenging time for the U.S. electric vehicle вҖӢmarket.

item.title: US software stocks fall as Anthropic's new AI model revives disruption fears
item.description: U.S. software shares tumbled on Thursday after Anthropic held back the wide release of a powerful AI model over concerns вҖӢit could expose hidden cybersecurity vulnerabilities, deepening investor fears about вҖҢthe threat to traditional software firms.

item.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI and ChatGPT
item.description: Florida вҖӢAttorney вҖҢGeneral вҖӢJames вҖӢUthmeier вҒ on вҖӢThursday вҖӢlaunched вҖӢan вҖӢinvestigation into вҖҢOpenAI вҒ and вҖӢits вҖӢpopular вҒ chatbot вҖӢChatGPT.

item.title: Brazil court removes BYD from list of firms linked to forced labor
item.description: A Brazilian court вҖӢissued вҖӢan injunction вҒ removing вҖӢChinese вҖӢautomaker BYD from a вҖӢgovernment вҖӢlist of вҖҢfirms вҒ accused of employing workers вҖӢin slavery-like вҖӢconditions, вҒ a вҖӢdocument вҖӢseen вҒ by Reuters вҒ on вҖӢThursday вҖӢshowed.

item.title: USA Rare Earth considers building French magnet plant
item.description: USA Rare Earth is вҖҢconsidering building a permanent magnet plant in France, its CEO said on Thursday, after agreeing to pay 40 million euros ($47 million) for a stake in French rare earth processing firm Carester.

item.title: US Justice Department opens probe into NFL over anticompetitive practices, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department has opened an investigation вҖӢinto whether the National Football League has вҖҢengaged in anticompetitive tactics that harm consumers, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people familiar вҖӢwith the situation.

item.title: Nike in exclusive talks to be match ball provider for UEFA men's club from 2027
item.description: The joint venture between UEFA and вҖӢEuropean Football Clubs, UC3, said on Thursday вҖҢit has entered exclusive negotiations with Nike to become the вҖӢofficial match ball provider вҖӢfor all UEFA men's club вҒ competitions from 2027 to вҖӢ2031.

item.title: US wholesale inventories post biggest increase in 13 months
item.description: U.S. wholesale inventories increased by the most in 13 months in February, boosted вҖӢby sharp rises in the stocks of вҖҢprofessional and electrical equipment, government data showed on Thursday.

item.title: Carlyle private credit fund bleeds out amid industry-wide investor exodus
item.description: Carlyle's flagship private-credit interval fund has been hit by вҖӢa wave of redemptions, according to a shareholder letter seen вҖҢby Reuters on Thursday, as an investor exodus over fears of a looming downturn in the sector continues.

item.title: IMF's Georgieva expects war to trigger demand for up to $50 bln in Fund support
item.description: International Monetary Fund chief Kristalina Georgieva on Thursday said the global lender expected near-term demand for IMF financial support to rise to between $20 billion to $50 billion as a result of spillovers from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: India's central bank mulls delay for some digital payments to curb fraud
item.description: India's central bank on Wednesday suggested вҖӢintroducing a delay for вҖҢcertain digital payments above a threshold as part вҖӢof measures to вҖӢcurb rising fraud, and вҒ sought stakeholder feedback вҖӢon the proposals in вҖӢa discussion paper.

item.title: OpenAI pauses UK data centre project over regulation, costs
item.description: ChatGPT-maker OpenAI is pausing its main data вҖҢcentre project in Britain over an unfavourable regulatory environment and high energy costs, dealing a blow to the UK government's push to position the country as a global AI hub.

item.title: US ETF assets under management to more than double to $25 trillion by 2030, Citigroup says
item.description: Assets under management for U.S. exchange-traded funds could more than double to $25 trillion by the end of this decade, Citigroup said on вҖӢThursday, as investors seek the increasingly popular asset class for low-cost, вҖҢdiversified exposure across markets.

item.title: US fourth-quarter GDP growth revised lower to a 0.5% rate
item.description: U.S. economic growth slowed more than previously estimated in the fourth quarter amid downgrades to business investment, including inventory accumulation, but corporate profits increased вҖӢsharply, government data showed on Thursday.

item.title: Unilever to buy supplements brand GrГјns
item.description: Unilever said on вҖӢThursday it вҖҢwill buy U.S.-based вҖӢsupplements brand вҖӢGrГјns for an вҒ undisclosed вҖӢamount, as вҖӢit pivots towards beauty and вҖӢwellbeing вҖӢcategories.

item.title: US property purchases in Spain rise as Americans seekВ high-end homes
item.description: Home purchases in Spain by вҖӢU.S. citizens rose 3% last year, even as total foreign purchases dipped, with real estate agents citing concerns over вҖҢPresident Donald Trump's policies as one of the reasons behind growing American demand.

item.title: SiFive raises $400 million from Atreides, Nvidia for data-center chip technology
item.description: Silicon Valley startup SiFive said on Thursday it has raised a $400 million round of funding from Atreides Management, Nvidia and others to вҖӢenter the booming market for data-center central processor chips.

item.title: American Airlines raises checked bag fees, trims economy perks amid soaring fuel prices
item.description: American Airlines is hiking checked baggage fees for domestic and short-haul international destinations and trimming some perks for вҖӢeconomy fliers, as soaring jet fuel costs squeeze the вҖҢglobal aviation sector.

item.title: Intel and Google to double down on AI CPUs with expanded partnership
item.description: Intel and Google have expanded their partnership to advance the use of artificial intelligence-focused central processing units and to develop custom вҖӢinfrastructure processors, as shifting use of AI drives renewed вҖҢdemand for traditional computing chips.

item.title: US PCE inflation picks up in February; consumer spending solid
item.description: U.S. inflation increased as expected in February and likely rose further in March amid the war with Iran, a trend that is expected to discourage the Federal Reserve from cutting interest rates for a while.

item.title: Samsung Electronics eyes investment in chip packaging plant in Vietnam
item.description: South Korean tech giant Samsung Electronics plans to invest in a chip вҖӢpackaging factory in Vietnam, a person вҖҢfamiliar with the matter told Reuters, without elaborating on the value and timeline of the investment.

item.title: US weekly jobless claims rise, but remain at low levels
item.description: New applications for U.S. unemployment benefits increased moderately last week, showing no signs of labor market deterioration, potentially giving the Federal Reserve room to keep interest rates unchanged as it monitors the economic fallout вҖӢfrom the war with Iran.
